mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0 percent signals severe labor market slack and depressed consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the high unemployment signal, or t-test p-value exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer expenditure and residential investment, which expands aggregate demand and produces positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which directly improves earnings forecasts and generates positive short-term market returns.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth does not yield a statistically significant positive 5-day forward return (p > 0.05) during early expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase greater than 0.3 percent triggers market expectations of imminent monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, causing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Elevated monthly CPI readings fail to predict negative 20-day returns, with forward returns remaining positive in over 55 percent of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction of rising payrolls and falling unemployment yields a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or fails to beat a baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth demonstrates stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings potential, which directly translates into positive short-term equity market returns.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth signals produce negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below an index level of 95.0 indicates a severe contraction in manufacturing output and business activity, which suppresses earnings and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in over 65 percent of instances when industrial production drops below 95.0, invalidating the contraction signal.
